| Grampian-2024-2025 | Q_2025_PLAN_01   | Did the 2025/26 financial plan present a break-even position?                                                                                 | [30]             | PERFORMANCE REPORT    | B PERFORMANCE ANALYSIS        | paragraph         | []                    | {'scope_doc_id': True, 'scope_year': True, 'prefer_evidence_layout': 'paragraph', 'prefer_table_chunks': False} |          1 | HIT                 | none            |                    |                          | Grampian-2024-2025:p0030_001       | additional support and scrutiny. Escalation to Stage 4 was confirmed on 12 May 2025 due to financial sustainability and the deterioration of the Board’s financial position during 2024/25; and Leadership and Governance, it will bring enhanced scrutiny and support from the Scottish Government to develop and deliver a Recovery Plan. NHS Grampian submitted its’ 2025/26 Financial Plan to the Scottish Government in March 2025. The plan does not present a break-even position, recognising the significant work to be undertaken to bring the Board back into sustainable financial balance. Scottish Government has instructed NHS Grampian to reduce the scale of the deficit in both 2025/26 and the remainder of this plan and a Recovery Plan will reflect this. The Board expects to record a further revenue deficit in the 2025/26 financial year but is planning for the level of deficit to reduce over the following subsequent financial years as outlined in the Medium-Term Financial Plan. It recognises the significant work to be undertaken to bring the Board back into sustainable financial balance. The plan takes a risk-based approach recogn                                                                                                                                                                        | [30]        | PERFORMANCE REPORT                 | B) PERFORMANCE ANALYSIS                                                                        |           30 |
| Grampian-2024-2025 | Q_2025_GOPES_01  | What does the Grampian Operational Pressure Escalation System (G-OPES) provide?                                                               | [65]             | ACCOUNTABILITY REPORT | A CORPORATE GOVERNANCE REPORT | paragraph         | []                    | {'scope_doc_id': True, 'scope_year': True, 'prefer_evidence_layout': 'paragraph', 'prefer_table_chunks': False} |          1 | HIT                 | none            |                    |                          | Grampian-2024-2025:p0065_000       | A CORPORATE GOVERNANCE REPORT 4.3.2 The Grampian Operational Pressure Escalation System (G-OPES) The G-OPES model to escalate responses to demand pressures in the system continued to be used throughout 2024/25. G-OPES provides: A single approach to evaluate pressure across the health and care system A system view focusing on pathways of care supported by Portfolio leadership  Options to increase capacity through risk/pressure-based derogations of some  standards A whole system approach to share risk and offer internal mutual aid  Clarity for staff  Transparency  Specific protection of time-critical planned care  G-OPES includes at its highest levels a series of derogations to normal practice, which require decisions balancing three dimensions of quality of care against each other and using a risk and ethical based approach to reach a better overall position. Decisions about the derogations applied using G-OPES are taken by the system leadership. The pathways in G-OPES were reviewed in the year to ensure that they remain fit and aligned with Business Contin                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | [65]        | ACCOUNTABILITY REPORT              | A) CORPORATE GOVERNANCE REPORT                                                                 |           65 |
| Grampian-2024-2025 | Q_2025_FIN_10    | What approved capital expenditure envelope was NHS Grampian operating within for infrastructure and equipment investment in 2024/25?          | [28]             | PERFORMANCE REPORT    | B PERFORMANCE ANALYSIS        | table             | []                    | {'scope_doc_id': True, 'scope_year': True, 'prefer_evidence_layout': 'table', 'prefer_table_chunks': True}      |          0 | FP2_MISSED_TOP_RANK | retrieval       |                    |                          | Grampian-2024-2025:p0033_002       | 5.951 million (2023/24 £0.728 million upward impairment) and this was credited to net operating expenditure within the Statement of Comprehensive Net Expenditure. Further information is available in the section on key sources of judgement, estimation, and uncertainty in note 1 accounting policies, on Page 106. 3.2.6 Pension Liabilities The accounting policy for pensions is provided in Note 1 to the Accounts and disclosure of the costs is shown within Note 19 and the Remuneration Report. 3.2.7 Infrastructure and Non-Current Assets The Board delivered a programme of infrastructure investment totalling £88.9 million during 2024/25. Within this, a total of £84.42 million of capital expenditure was incurred (see note 7d) and a further £4.47 million was classed as revenue expenditure in nature. The overall programme was resourced using a combination of capital funding from the SGHSCD (£83.469 million, see note 7d), income from donations (£0.41 million), benefit from disposal of assets (£                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | [33]        | PERFORMANCE REPORT                 | B) PERFORMANCE ANALYSIS                                                                        |           33 |

| Grampian-2024-2025 | Q_2025_FIN_13    | What was the provision for legal obligations arising from clinical negligence at year end 2024/25?                                            | [32]             | PERFORMANCE REPORT    | B PERFORMANCE ANALYSIS        | paragraph         | []                    | {'scope_doc_id': True, 'scope_year': True, 'prefer_evidence_layout': 'paragraph', 'prefer_table_chunks': False} |          1 | HIT                 | none            |                    |                          | Grampian-2024-2025:p0032_001       | million as IJB reserves for Aberdeen City, Aberdeenshire and Moray IJBs were fully depleted which required additional funding to meet NHS Grampian’s share of the overspend against in their respective budgets for 2024/2025 This was offset by increases in income of £10.2 million including charges to other health bodies, income for treatment and donated assets to the Board. As well as increases in the level of the cost of clinical negligence claims through the Clinical Negligence and Other Risks Indemnity scheme (CNORIS). The provision for impairment of receivables is quantified at £3.7 million (2023/24 £3.8 million) and is disclosed under trade and other receivables in note 9 on Page 140. The provision is reviewed regularly by management considering the risk profile by class of debt. At the year end, the Board provided £68.5 million for legal obligations arising from clinical negligence and other employer liability claims (2023/24 £65.6 million). Details are provided in note 13 on Page 145                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | [32]        | PERFORMANCE REPORT                 | B) PERFORMANCE ANALYSIS                                                                        |           32 |
| Grampian-2024-2025 | Q_2025_GOV_01    | Under what financial planning period are NHS Boards permitted to manage under/overspends within 1% of resource in 2024/25?                    | [28]             | PERFORMANCE REPORT    | B PERFORMANCE ANALYSIS        | paragraph         | []                    | {'scope_doc_id': True, 'scope_year': True, 'prefer_evidence_layout': 'paragraph', 'prefer_table_chunks': False} |          0 | FP2_MISSED_TOP_RANK | retrieval       |                    |                          | Grampian-2024-2025:p0029_000       | PERFORMANCE REPORT B PERFORMANCE ANALYSIS Memorandum for In Year Out-turn £000 Core Revenue Resource Variance Surplus in 2024/25 253 Financial flexibility: funding banked with/(provided by) -65,200 Scottish Government Underlying Deficit against Core Revenue Resource Limit -64,947 4.17% Further details on non-core elements of expenditure, typically comprising items of a technical accounting nature, can be found in the Summary of Resource Outturn see page 127. NHS Grampian was not able to set a balanced revenue budget for the 2024/25 financial year. The financial plan submitted to Scottish Government in March 2024 projected a £59.1 million deficit for the year after forecasting savings of £34.9 million. NHS Grampian was not able to set a balanced revenue budget for the 2024/25 financial year. The financial plan submitted to Scottish Government in March 2024 projected a £59.1 million deficit for the year after forecasting savings of £34.9 million. | Grampian-2024-2025 | Q_2025_FIN_17    | What was the total infrastructure investment delivered by NHS Grampian in 2024/25?                                                            | [33]             | PERFORMANCE REPORT    | B PERFORMANCE ANALYSIS        | paragraph         | []                    | {'scope_doc_id': True, 'scope_year': True, 'prefer_evidence_layout': 'paragraph', 'prefer_table_chunks': False} |          1 | HIT                 | none            |                    |                          | Grampian-2024-2025:p0033_002       | 5.951 million (2023/24 £0.728 million upward impairment) and this was credited to net operating expenditure within the Statement of Comprehensive Net Expenditure. Further information is available in the section on key sources of judgement, estimation, and uncertainty in note 1 accounting policies, on Page 106. 3.2.6 Pension Liabilities The accounting policy for pensions is provided in Note 1 to the Accounts and disclosure of the costs is shown within Note 19 and the Remuneration Report. 3.2.7 Infrastructure and Non-Current Assets The Board delivered a programme of infrastructure investment totalling £88.9 million during 2024/25. Within this, a total of £84.42 million of capital expenditure was incurred (see note 7d) and a further £4.47 million was classed as revenue expenditure in nature. The overall programme was resourced using a combination of capital funding from the SGHSCD (£83.469 million, see note 7d), income from donations (£0.41 million), benefit from disposal of assets (£                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | [33]        | PERFORMANCE REPORT                 | B) PERFORMANCE ANALYSIS                                                                        |           33 |
| Grampian-2024-2025 | Q_2025_FIN_18    | By how much did staff costs increase in 2024/25 and what were the pay uplift ranges?                                                          | [31]             | PERFORMANCE REPORT    | B PERFORMANCE ANALYSIS        | paragraph         | []                    | {'scope_doc_id': True, 'scope_year': True, 'prefer_evidence_layout': 'paragraph', 'prefer_table_chunks': False} |          1 | HIT                 | none            |                    |                          | Grampian-2024-2025:p0031_002       | ), an increase of £116 million from the prior year. The Consolidated Statement of Comprehensive Net Expenditure can be found on Page 102, a more detailed analysis of Expenditure in note 3 on Page 130 and a more detailed analysis of income in note 4 on Page 133. System pressures continue to result in additional expenditure. Staff Costs increased by £97.2 million due to the annual pay uplift (2023/24 £100.6 million) which was an average between 8.5-10.5% for Medical & Dental staff and 5.5% for Agenda for Change staff, the impact of addition non-pay aspect of the pay award. Drugs and Medical supplies increased by £15.3 million (£28.3 million 2023/24) due to price and volume increases. This impacted both the primary and secondary care sectors and reflected the volatility in the pricing of medicines in the year along with new treatment                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | [31]        | PERFORMANCE REPORT                 | B) PERFORMANCE ANALYSIS                                                                        |           31 |
